channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Abortion opponents shift focus to pills with lawsuits, proposed laws and possible federal action
item.description: The battles over abortion in the U.S. are increasingly focusing on the pills that are now the most common way pregnancies are ended.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The new right celebrates Trump at a conservative conference and sidesteps inconsistencies
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — As Washington heaved over the possibility of a partial government shutdown, leading far-right figures gathered with thousands of Donald Trump’s most ardent supporters and, for the most part, gloried in splintering the president-elect’s party.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican, US police deploy more officers at border for holiday travel
item.description: Lately, it's been much more dangerous to travel to Mexico, because of drug cartels working along the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Hochul slammed for saying she's made subways safer on same day woman burned alive on train
item.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul was called out after she praised how safe the New York City subway system has purportedly become, just hours after a woman was burned alive on a train.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driver killed by police after driving truck through Texas mall, injuring 5
item.description: Authorities said the driver traveled "several hundred yards" through a JCPenney, injuring multiple people.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, ABC 4

item.title: Pickup truck driver killed by police after driving through busy mall store is identified
item.description: KILLEEN, Texas (AP) — A pickup truck driver fleeing police careened through the doors of a JCPenney store at a busy Texas mall, injuring five people before he was fatally shot by officers, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 174 people rescued after Winter Park Resort gondola malfunction
item.description: Over 150 people were rescued Saturday after a gondola malfunction at Winter Park Resort, a spokesperson confirmed to FOX31.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Houston Texans wide receiver Tank Dell released from hospital after severe knee injury
item.description: It’s the second straight year Tank Dell has sustained what is almost certain to be a season-ending injury.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Virginia woman faces jail time after practicing illegal dentistry
item.description: A Virginia woman has been convicted of practicing dentistry without a license, following accusations that she illegally performed veneer procedures at her tooth jewelry business.
item.dc:creator: Karina F. Garcia, KRON4

item.title: New York City police apprehend suspect in the death of a woman found on fire in a subway car
item.description: New York City police say they have taken into custody a person of interest in the death of a woman who may have fallen asleep on a stationary subway train before being lit on fire by a man she didn’t know.
item.dc:creator: Susan Haigh, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What are 'snow rollers'? Photos of rare phenomenon in Michigan spark curiosity
item.description: “This is the coolest thing I've ever seen,” one viewer wrote, remarking on the dozens of doughnut-shaped snowballs peppered across a field.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NORAD's Santa tracker was a Cold War morale boost. Now it attracts millions of kids
item.description: The Christmas tradition has become nearly global in scope: Children from around the world track Santa Claus as he sweeps across the earth, delivering presents and defying time.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Mystery drones could be identified faster using new detection tool, but FAA lacks resources
item.description: The FAA's new "digital license plate" for drones has yet to be rolled out to law enforcement, hindering efforts to identify mystery drones in the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: O.J. Simpson prosecutor Marcia Clark dissects evolving media's impact on trials and true crime
item.description: O.J. Simpson prosecutor Marcia Clark revives the forgotten 1950s murder trial of Barbara "Bloody Babs" Graham and discusses decades of evolving true crime coverage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top-ranking NYPD officer abruptly resigns amid sexual misconduct allegations
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The highest-ranking uniformed officer in the New York Police Department has resigned amid allegations he demanded sex from a subordinate in exchange for opportunities to earn extra pay.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Veteran who lost both legs shares his hopeful story for the holidays
item.description: One month later, on Thanksgiving Day, Gabe called Kayla with the devastating news that he had been injured by an improvised explosive device blast and med-evacuated out with one of his best friends. Both men lost their legs.
item.dc:creator: Kim Posey, NewsNation

item.title: Over 600,000 Nuna Rava car seats recalled over possible crash failure
item.description: Nuna Baby Essentials is recalling more than 600,000 of its Rava line of car seats over a harness defect that could cause the restraint system to fail in a crash.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: FDA: Contaminated cucumbers linked to 100 salmonella cases; Recall tied to Missouri and Illinois
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration reports that contaminated cucumbers are linked to 100 salmonella cases across 23 states, following a recall that included products sold in Missouri and Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, NewsNation

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, WGN 9

item.title: Mahomes throws for TD and runs for score as Chiefs beat Texans 27-19 to close in on No. 1 seed
item.description: Patrick Mahomes knew he could run on the ankle he sprained last week. The bigger concern for the Chiefs quarterback was how he would stop, when all the weight and pressure and momentum would land squarely upon it.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A rocket from Yemen strikes Tel Aviv, injuring 16, and Palestinians mourn a dozen children in Gaza
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — A rocket fired from Yemen hit an area of Tel Aviv overnight, leaving 16 people injured by shattered glass, the Israeli military said Saturday, days after Israeli airstrikes hit Houthi rebels who have been launching missiles in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and WAAFA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Blake Lively accuses 'It Ends With Us' director Justin Baldoni of harassment, smear campaign
item.description: In the complaint, Lively accuses Baldoni and the studio of embarking on a “multi-tiered plan” to damage her reputation following a meeting in which she and her husband Ryan Reynolds addressed “repeated sexual harassment and other disturbing behavior” by Baldoni and a producer on the movie.
item.dc:creator: Mallika Sen, Fox 5

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Daughter of notorious 'pom-pom mom' says cheerleading murder plot nearly destroyed her life
item.description: In 1991, Wanda Holloway was charged with conspiring to kill the mother of her daughter’s rival. Holloway's daughter, Shanna, is speaking out in a new true crime docuseries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canyon de Chelly in Arizona will become latest national park unit to ban commercial air tours
item.description: CANYON DE CHELLY NATIONAL MONUMENT, Ariz. (AP) — Commercial air tours will soon be prohibited over Canyon de Chelly National Monument in northeastern Arizona under a plan approved this week by the Federal Aviation Administration and the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Father accused of beheading son in Sacramento County
item.description: (FOX40.COM) -- Sacramento County deputies arrested a 28-year-old man on Friday morning in connection with the death of his 1-year-old son. Andrey Demskiy, 28, is accused of beheading his son.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: Schools are also warning that international students should travel with additional documentation of their connection to their American college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WGN 9

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Report alleges Coast Guard leaders kept sexual assault investigation secret
item.description: HARTFORD, Conn. (AP) — Former top Coast Guard officials hid a yearslong investigation into sexual assault and harassment at the service's academy from both Congress and the public after leaders debated the fallout from a potential disclosure, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana man sentenced to the maximum of 130 years in prison for 2017 killings of 2 teenage girls
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls who vanished during a winter hike was sentenced to a maximum of 130 years in prison Friday in the case that’s long cast a shadow over the teens’ small hometown of Delphi. Allen, 52, was convicted on Nov.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
